title: Mouthwatering Thai Egg Tom Yum
description: A bright, savory soup that balances sweet, spicy, and crunchy with a fresh mustard kick and char-grilled Yang seasoning. This ovo‑vegetarian tom yum packs crowd‑pleasing depth and a satisfying crunch.
instructions: In a pot, combine vegetable broth, water, kaffir lime leaves, galangal, and lemongrass; bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat and let the aromatics infuse for 5 minutes; then strain out solids.
Return the simmering broth to low heat; stir in fish sauce, lime juice, and sweet chili sauce until well mixed.
Add toasted mustard seeds and bamboo shoots to the broth; simmer for another 3 minutes.
Crack the eggs into a small bowl, then gently slide them into the soup to poach.
Cook the eggs for 2 minutes until whites are set but yolks remain runny; remove the pot from heat.
Stir in julienned carrots for crunch and heat through for 30 seconds.
Ladle soup into bowls, garnish with fresh cilantro and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds, and serve hot.
